When the number of token is 1, per-token scale is [[1]], per-tensor scale is [1] or ().
This issue indirectly causes
when we use aiter to perform preshuffle on weight, if the number of tokens in inference equals 1, aiter's bpreshuffle will not be invoked correctly, resulting in garbled output.

Purpose

To avoid this kind of mess and breakage, this PR

vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
#26315 added the AttentionConfig object and --attention-config CLI argument, but did not add the attention_config argument to the LLM() class explicitly. Therefore, it was handled by the kwargs, which works fine except when explicitly passing attention_config=None. This PR handles this case

vllm.title: [BUILD] use sm_100f when compiling flashmla to fix support on sm103
vllm.author: Harry-Chen
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose
Now flashmla is compiled with CUDA arch 9.0a and 10.0a. This will trigger errors on B300 (sm103a).
This PR uses the new family specifier 10.0f introduced in CUDA 12.9 when possible, which will support all possible 10.x computing capabilities.
Besides, the original version CUDA checking was w

models (e.g., Qwen), even though the streaming delta events contained correct content.
Problem
When using the Responses API with stream=True, the streaming events (response.out

vllm.title: [responsesAPI][8] input/output messages for ResponsesParser
vllm.author: qandrew
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Purpose

add input/output messages to ResponsesParser. If we have a 2 turn conversation (user input 1, output 1, MCP call, input 2, output 2), we'll have user input 1 in response.input_messages, and [output 1, input 2, output 2] in response.output_messages).
refactor make_response_output_items_from_
